essay: "Home schooling become popular. Home schooling is a form of education children are taught at home by their parents or tutors instead of attend a traditional school. In the following essay, i will discuss the reasons for this learn way. Home schooling is a good way to learn for several reasons. Most importantly, it helps us gain a deeper understanding of what we learn. If we don’t get something, we can quickly understand it because we are in familiar environment where we can be more active. Home schooling also creates more relax learning schedule, allowing us study at every time we prefer. It helps us don’t pressure about set study hours. Morever, that home schooling should be carefully consider. Learning at home to much will make some metal disorder like autism, poor communication skills, ... Stay at home too much, children can be depend on their family or tutor. Home schooling should be limited, just use that learn way for special option or student who know what they doing. In conclusion therefore, home schooling have many benefit but if we can attend a private, we should go."

"It is often say that hobbies and interests change mainly because of fashion and trends, not because what people want to do in their free time. I complete disagree with this opinion think that most hobbies are chosen for personal reasons rather than social influence. The first reason is that hobbies usually come from people own personality and passion. For example, someone enjoy reading books or painting pictures often continues these activities for many years, no matter if they are popular or not. These hobbies give people happy, relax, and a way to express themselves. Fashion trends may attract attention for a short time, but they can not replace the deep satisfy people get from personal interests. The second reason is that many hobbies need effort, time, or money, so people must really enjoy them to continue. For example, play football, learn the guitar, or swim all require regular practice. If people are only follow a fashion trend, they would probably stop after a short period. On the hand, when people has a real passion, they are motivated to spend energy and stay committed. This show that lasting hobbies are more about true enjoyment than about fashionable. I strongly believe that hobbies mainly chosen based on what people enjoy, not on changing trends, because they reflect personality."

"It is often say that patient is not good and people should act right away instead of wait. I do not agree with this idea because patience often better than acting too fast. First, act no patience can bring mistakes and bad results. Many big choices need time to collect enough facts and think of different ways. For example, when choose a job or using money, fast act may cause loss. On the other hand, patient people usually make better choices that lead to success in the long run. Second, patience help us build self-control and hard work, it very important in life. Many goals, like learning a new language or making a job, can not be done in one day. For example, students study step by step with patient often do better than those who want fast results and give up soon. That is why patient is a way bring steady grow. All at all, I believe patient is a good and useful plan. Even though quick action is sometimes needed, in case patient helps people make wiser choices and get better results."

"Nowadays, many countries have people from different nations live together. Some people think this is good because it makes country more interesting and it grow faster. I mosly agree with this idea for some reason. Firstly, when people from different country live in one place, they bring new cultures, languages, and food. This make the country more colorful and exciting. For example, in big city like London, you can see many cultures and eat food from India, China, Italy. It helps people learn about the world without leave their home. Also, different cultures can teach people how to respect and understand together better. Secondly, when a country has many nationalities, it can develop quick. People from different places bring new ideas and skills. They may have different ways to solve problems or do business. This can help the country become more creative and grow faster in areas like science, technology, economy. There many nationalities also have some challenges. Sometimes, conflicts because misunderstanding or different believe. People are not feel united as one nation. Sum up, I think having people from different countries is good for a country. It makes life more interesting and help the country to develop faster. But it is also important to make sure everyone respect each other and live in peace"

"Many people believe that computers is greatest invention in the 20th century which now humanity consider important in life. I strongly convince with that idea and in this essay, I will discuss some following reasons. Firstly, computers allow to store large amounts of data which help people access the information faster. As a result, study and work will be convenient and efficient. Secondly, computers help us relax after hard-studying days. For example, I often listen to my favourite songs or catch my eyes on some interesting videos on youtube, instagram or tiktok. Thirdly, it’s easy and convenient for me to make friends with different people in the world not care distance and language. For instance, I can easy chat with my foreign friends by call or send emails everyday by the computer through the internet. In conclusion, there is no doubt that computer is constributing to the society development positive and study, work or entertain among them. My view is that we should use the computer for good purpose."

"Nowadays, people demand for foreign country is increase, so there are some cultural regulations in that country. While some people think that visitors to other nationals need to respect and follow local customs. However, the rest disagree and believe that the host country should accept many various culturals. On the one hand, many people claim that the government should not limit any cultural for many reasons. Because many people would travel abroad for their holiday and fun activities. Some foreign tourists, especially Europeans, likes to be free and not subjects to any rules. It is for these reasons lead to cultural and political conflicts. On the other hand, there are several reason why other people and I agree that visitors should follow local customs and behavior. First of all, join voluntarily, tourist visiting a certain country or place need to follow the cultural regulations of that place. This show the respect of visitors to the culture of the place and helps themselves have a positive view in the eyes of others, especially local residents. Secondly, when tourist immerse themselves in those cultures, it helps them bring a new feeling, a new experience and learn many interesting things to add to their own knowledge manual. In conclusion, although it is most important to follow the local cultural regulations, free dom and openness are also important factors help attract tourists from all over the world to travel."

"Recently, the model of family has been different. There are some changes of numbers, duties and responsibilities. In my opinion, these developments are positive. While it was common in the past for members of some families to had a lot of children and live together under one roof, nowadays many people tend to live in a nuclear family structure. When family scale becomes smaller, it reduce the possible of discord among family. This is a good change everyone will enjoy the feeling of privacy and coziness. Besides, people living a small family units can decrease costs and easy handle issues related to budget. They become more finance stable. On the other hand, the most biggest change over the past decade has been the increase freedom of women and equality between husband and wife. In the past, men the breadwinners in most families, while women stayed at home to cook, clean, and raise children, and have no obligation or power in the home. Today, both parents can become the breadwinners of the family, and in many cases, some fathers stay at home to help support the family and retire as a solid backing for their partner to continue to thrive career. This is good change because women have opportunity to pursue their own careers and earn money. In addition, some of their husbands’ families become stay-at-home dads who take care of their children and housework. In summary, I believe these trends are positive the family structure as well as the role of the family has changed a lot to bring about a nice and cozy life, there is no longer the concept of male contempt for women."

"Nowadays, many people mention that cook and eat at their own places is better for them and their families than eat outside. I totally agree this and I will discuss this issue for the following reasons in this essay. To begin with, I believe that cooking at home save money more than having a meal in the restaurant. For example, a family can buy almost basic cuisine such as meat, pork, vegetables, fruits,... only with 100.000 VN Dong and can cook for the whole week. By contrast, if eat at the street food vendors or canteens, it only takes two days to spend money. Therefore, I believe that homemade food will save more money and easier to control money. On the other hand, making food at home will ensure foodstuff safety and eating at home is healthier. For instance, when you go out to eat, you won's know if the food is clean and safe or not or they add chemicals to the food to make it fresh like new. By contrast, at home, you can choose many fresh and healthy foods for youself and your family at a mid-range prices. In addition, cooking and eating at home also connect family kins together. They cook, eat and talk together to in the category. In conclusion, having meal outside more expensive than preparing and processing food at home. Spending time to learn to cook many delicious dishes for yourself save your expenses and emotional connection family members."

"These days, many businesses increase the number of plastic bags and bottles from their products, lead to the environment becomes polluted. In this essay, I give my reason and how the government need to act on this issue. Overall, it is clear that there are some methods to explaining the cause of this problem. The first most is that humans still have poor conscious. They reckon that it is just a small action, so it not affect nature. But actually, the environment is strong affected by people bad habits on a one-to-one basis. Another clear point is that the number of public residents increase, with around 8 billion people in the world. As a result is an increase the consumption of household equipments. Factories and buyers ignore this issue instead of look for another choices for reduce trash. On the other hand, government need to take action to prevent this situation. First of all, they need to heavy tax punishment for factories, people who law-breaking about the environment. People working in government agencies should strict manage resources and waste treatment systems. This because these methods can reduce toxic chemicals before relieve them direct to nature. Lastly, they must sign contracts the green projects or events with develop countries. In conclusion, this is a global issue, it is not only the responsible of individuals but also businesses and the office."

"Some people think that we shouldn’t spend money to save animals are in danger of disappear. They think it is waste money, and we should use money for other thing. But I don’t agree with this idea. I think it is important to save animals. One reason why we should save them is that animals are important for nature. They all have a job to do. If one animal disappear, it can make big problems for other animals and plants. For example, if we don’t have bees, it will difficult to grow food because bees help to move pollar from one plant to another. Another reason: we have a duty to protect these animals. It is not right to let they die. Many animals in danger because what humans do, like cut down trees or pollute the water. So, we need to help them survive. Some animals are important for our culture. For example, the tiger is symbol power in some country. Some people say that we should use the money to help people instead, like build hospitals or schools. Of course, help people is very important. But we can do both things. We can help people and save animals. Saving animals can also help people, for example, by bringing tourists to our country, and this can create jobs. In conclusion, I think it is important to save animals that are in danger. They are important for nature, and we have a duty to protect them. It might cost money, but it is worth it. We need to find ways to help both people and animals so that we can have a better future."

"Nowadays, home-schooling is becoming more popular in many countries. However, I believe that attending at state or private school is more beneficial than studying at home. In the following essay, I will discuss the reasons for this view. One major reason why home-schooling is popular is that students can study at their own speed. Some students learn faster or slower than other, and studying at home allows them to spend more time on subjects which they don’t understand. Another reason could be that home-schooling gives students a quieter and less stressful learning environment than crowded schools. It helps learners to concentrate on the lesson because of a quiet and suitable learning environment. Home-schooling also has some other plus points. Students can choose study schedule. They can focus more on subjects they are interested in and feel more comfortable when they are learning. In addition, parents can also support their children more closely, which helps students improve their study results. However, I think that attending a private or state school is better. At school, students can meet numerous people and make friends. They also learn how to communicate and work in groups. Moreover, schools offer many activities such as sports, clubs and events - music, art and crafts, sports, food, festivals to name some, which help students develop social skills or more confident. In conclusion, home-schooling is becoming popular because it offers flexible learning and a comfortable environment. However, personally, I think studying at school is more beneficial for students’ education and social skills."

"Nowadays, hobbies and interests of people often change quickly. Some people say that this is only because of fashion and trends, not what people really want. Personally, I partly agree with this opinion. On the one hand, it is true that many hobbies are affected by fashion. For example, when TikTok or Instagram shows a new sport or game, many young people try it just because it is popular. They may not really enjoy it, but they do it to follow friends or to post pictures online. This means that some hobbies are not from real interest, but from social pressure and modern trends. On the other hand, I also believe that hobbies can show what individuals truly like. Even if a trend starts from fashion, people only keep doing it if they find real happiness. For instance, some people begin cooking because it becomes popular online, but later they continue it because they really enjoy preparing food for family. In this case, hobbies are more than a trend; they reflect personal preference. In conclusion, I think that hobbies are influenced by trends and fashion at first, but in the long term, they also show what people really want to do in their free time."

"In recent years, the problem of overweight and unhealthy children has become a serious concern for society. While some people believe that governments should take full responsibility, I argue that families also play an important role in solving this issue. This essay will discuss both views before presenting my opinion. On the one hand, governments can strongly influence children’s health. One reason is that they can provide education and raise awareness about the dangers of unhealthy lifestyles through schools and media. For instance, television programs and campaigns can show children how to eat balanced meals and exercise regularly. Another reason is that governments have the power to make laws. They can ban advertisements for junk food, improve the quality of school lunches, and ensure that physical education is part of the curriculum. With these actions, children are more likely to develop healthy habits from an early age. On the other hand, families have direct responsibility for shaping their children’s lifestyles. Parents are the ones who prepare meals at home and decide what children eat every day. By offering fruits, vegetables, and home-cooked food instead of fast food, they encourage better eating habits. In addition, families can set routines that include outdoor activities, limit screen time, and ensure enough sleep. Most importantly, parents should be good role models. When children see their parents eating healthily, exercising, and living a balanced life, they are more likely to follow. To sum up, both governments and families ought to share responsibility for reducing childhood obesity and unhealthy lifestyles. However, I strongly believe that families have a greater duty because they influence children’s daily choices and long-term habits."

"It is often said that a country becomes more vibrant and grows faster when it has a diverse population of different nationalities. I completely agree with this idea and believe that cultural diversity brings many social and economic benefits that help a country develop more effectively. Firstly, having people from different countries makes a nation more culturally rich and interesting. Each group brings its own traditions, food, language, and festivals, which create a colorful and dynamic society. For example, multicultural cities like Toronto or London are known for their variety of cultural experiences, which attract tourists and create a more open-minded and inclusive community. Therefore, a mix of nationalities adds to the country’s charm and global appeal. Secondly, diversity in the population can boost economic growth and innovation. When people from different backgrounds work together, they can share unique ideas and approaches to problem-solving. This often leads to creativity in business, science, and technology. For instance, many successful companies today, such as Google or Microsoft, have diverse teams made up of people from various nationalities. These teams bring new perspectives that help businesses grow and adapt to a global market. Thus, a multicultural population plays an important role in national development. In conclusion, I believe that a mixture of nationalities not only makes a country more interesting but also helps it develop faster. Cultural richness and diverse talents contribute greatly to both social harmony and economic progress."

"Numerous people believe that computers are the most important invention of the last century. While it is agreed that computers play a key role in today’s world, I would agree that there are a couple of inventions that are far more crucial. It can be said that computers are an indispensable part of our lives for some reasons. Firstly, these gadget devices can store big data, thus support people to study and research easily. For instance, students can stay at home searching for thousands of online courses instead of taking extra lessons. Secondly, they help people talk and connect with each other more easily and conveniently. Typical examples are meetings between companies which can be conducted through a video calling application. However, I am of the opinion that there are a few more important inventions in the last century. The first reason for my argument is that we have created tablets. In fact, tablets are useful for users because they are small in size, lighter than computer, full of basic tasks, some types come with a keyboard to make it more convenient to use. Another reason is that the development of airplane is also a big breakthrough. This is mainly because it helps passengers save travel time and better protect health by avoiding fatigue as well as accidents. Thes passengers can also watch the blue sky during the day and the bustling city scene at night.In summary, computers have been contributing a significant part to people’s lives in work, study, research and entertainment, helping society to develop and expand. "

"It is the case that people have different views about how tourists should behave when travelling to other countries. While some people encourage that the host country should welcome with different and more particular cultures, others deem that visitors should respect this country’s traditions and pay attention to the rules of the territory. This essay tries to discuss both views. On the one hand, they opine that people may be interested in other countries’ cultures when tourists come to these destinations and by this way they forget their local customs. The government desires tourists to realize the diversity of their country, hence they transfer the customs of other territories into their country. For example, a new style of wearing would be alternatived to local clothes or new kinds of food would be developed instead of traditional ones. Therefore, they are potentially losing own cultural identity. On the other hand, some others worry about their local customs and traditional cultures and they think that bringing new cultures and behaviours to one country by visitors can pose a serious challenge for their cultures. Not only the presidents but also the residents have to value their traditions and have a desire to preserve it. When tourists visit, they have a responsibility to take care of it. They must abide the same legislations as the indigenous residents, such as traffic laws and respect territory rights. Actually, numerous countries are competing together for being the destination of visitors in holidays and without respecting different cultures, they may be lost in this competition. In a nutshell, host countries can make a potential for tourists to be free and also encourage them to follow local rules and customs by introducing and showing their benefits. Therefore, visitors would be interested in behaving as similarly as local people."

"It is the case that the patterns of family have greatly diversified. While there are some drawbacks related to this matter, I think that the benefits are much greater. On the one hand, it is obvious that there are a number of significant disadvantages in relation to families. One of the most evident demerits is that it is increasingly difficult for family members to spend quality time together. For example, in most households, both parents are now compelled to work full-time to sustain their life, unlike the past when the sole responsibility of providing for the family rested on the father's shoulders. Furthermore, this shift has resulted in increasing pressure on parents to balance work and family life, often leading to the neglect of their children. Another negative aspect that should not be overlooked is that women’s chances of accessing high-paying jobs are still lower than men’s. For instance, the proportion of women in management and leadership has increased, although remains low. The consequences of this are long-lasting, affecting not only the individual family but also the broader society. On the other hand, I think that the positively far reaching outnumber the risks involved. To begin with, it is undoubted that there is also a significant change in terms of the roles of the father and the mother in a family. The responsibility of males as the breadwinner of the family has now been mitigated by sharing with their wife, and now women have equal working opportunities to do the jobs she loves. As a result, household duties are also shared equally between the couple. For example, women may reap the benefit of having their own career and thus financial freedom. Men have been freed from the financial pressure of being the only source of income in the family. Another potential benefit is that gender equality allows them to be open and true to themselves without discrimination. They are equal in all fields of politics, economy, culture, society and family, supported and facilitated. Therefore, they extremely have equal opportunities to participate in the development and enjoy the fruits of evolution.  In conclusion, all the justifications above has provided a concrete foundation that these changes are beneficial for all members of the family."

"Numerous people argue that some individuals who do not finish school can become more successful than those who complete their education. This situation makes people wonder about the reasons and what is really needed to achieve success in life. On the one hand, there are several explanations why dropouts sometimes do better. First of all, some famous people like Bill Gates or Steve Jobs had strong ideas and creativity that school could not give them. They had motivation to try something new, while many students just follow the same path. Secondly, leaving school early sometimes push people into real life earlier, so they learn from experience and become more independent. For example, some young entrepreneurs start their own business without waiting for graduation, and they can achieve success more quickly. On the other hand, becoming successful is not only about dropping out. It usually requires many qualities. Hard work and persistence are very important because without them, talent cannot last long. Also, people need confidence and ability to take risks, since success never comes without challenges.In addition, having good communication skills and sometimes a bit of luck also play a role. Without these, even a well-educated person may not reach success. In conclusion, some people who leave school can become very successful due to creativity and early experience. However, to achieve success in general, people need determination, effort, and the courage to follow their own way."

"It is often argued that raising the minimum legal age for driving cars or riding motorbikes is more effective than other methods to ensure road safety. I completely disagree with this opinion and think that education and stricter traffic laws are more important than simply increasing the legal driving age. Firstly, increasing the minimum age does not guarantee that young drivers will drive more carefully when they reach the new age limit. Road safety depends more on drivers’ knowledge, awareness, and responsibility than on age alone. For example, a well-trained in 18-year-old may drive safely than an untrained 40-year-old. Instead of raising the age, the government should provide better driving lessons in communities to teach young people about traffic rules and safe driving skills. This would help reduce accidents more effectively. Secondly, stricter traffic laws and punishments would encourage all drivers, regardless of age, to follow the rules. Many accidents happen because people break speed limits, drive after drinking alcohol, or use their phones while driving. For instance, in many countries, drivers must pay a lot of money or even lose their license if they break the law. These strict rules have helped reduce accidents. Therefore, improving law enforcement and raising people’s awareness are better solutions than increasing the legal driving age. In conclusion, I believe that raising the minimum legal age for driving is not the best way to improve road safety, because education and stricter laws are much more effective in encouraging responsible driving and reducing accidents."

"In the contemporary context, becoming a patient person is one of the most debated topics in our society. Some groups of people argue that it is not only unnecessary to be patient but also advisable to act fastly, whilst I mainly disagree with this point of view. On the one hand, it can be obviously seen that there are a variety of reasons why people have become more impatient than they were before. Firstly, people are under pressure from both time and workload. The earlier they finish, the more salary and leisure time they earn for themselves. Secondly, another reason for this issue is that there are more and more individuals who follow procrastination. Maybe the pressure could lead others to regularly postpone or delay their task so as to procrastinate until the last minute. As a result, they could not either complete perfectly or have significant mistakes which they might not be able to fix. They will be hasty in the next steps they take action. On the other hand, there are numerous benefits related to human beings learn and practice the precious virtues, especially patience. To begin with, as for a person who has the patience of a saint, he will calm his nerves and understand thoroughly in any situations confronting him. For example, a patient teacher acts gently and sympathizes with her junior students’ wrong actions instead of getting angry and yelling at them. Following that, being patient is one of the optimal approaches while we are in trouble. In this case, we need to keep us cool and think about the solution to avoid making significant errors. Finally, adolescences should cultivate patience thorough adults’s behaviors and remind each other frequently. In conclusion, there are two sides to everything and the virtue of calm is not the exception. It is imperative that we should improve ourselves and grow up by controlling our mind and not losing patience with anything."

"Home-schooling is becoming a common choice for many families around the world. While traditional education remains dominant, an increasing number of parents are opting to educate their children at home. In this essay, I will discuss the reasons for this trend and argue that while home-schooling has benefits, attending a traditional school is generally more advantageous for a child's holistic development. There are several reasons why home-schooling is gaining popularity. Most importantly, it allows parents to control the learning environment and curriculum. Some parents feel that local schools do not meet their child’s specific needs or that the teaching quality is insufficient. Furthermore, safety concerns, such as bullying or negative peer pressure in schools, often drive parents to withdraw their children and teach them in the safety of their own homes. It is true that home-schooling offers distinct benefits compared to state or private schools. The most significant advantage is flexibility. Children can learn at their own pace, focusing deeply on subjects they enjoy without the pressure of a rigid timetable. Additionally, the one-on-one attention from a parent ensures that the child understands concepts thoroughly, which is difficult to achieve in a crowded classroom.It is important to note, however, that attending a school provides crucial opportunities that home-schooling lacks. Schools are not just about academics; they are vital for socialization. Interacting with peers, navigating friendships, and dealing with conflicts teach children essential life skills. Moreover, schools provide access to specialized facilities like science labs, sports fields, and libraries, as well as subject-matter experts that parents usually cannot replicate at home. To conclude, home-schooling is popular due to its flexibility and safe environment. However, I believe that attending a private or state school is more beneficial overall. The social interaction and access to professional resources available in a school setting are indispensable for preparing children for the real world."

"In the contemporary era, leisure activities have undergone noticeable alterations, with numerous youngsters gravitating towards fashionable trends rather than pursuing timeless hobbies. While a minority of individuals still suppose that interests come from real passion, I firmly believe that they are more often shaped by social fashions and temporary movements. On the one hand, it is undeniable that personal hobbies still motivate some individuals. For example, certain teenagers stick with drawing or playing chess because of family influence or their own curiosity. If practiced regularly, these hobbies not only help them build their own identity but also enhance patience and concentration, Therefore, true passion still exists but less ubiquitous. On the other hand, it is obvious that most hobbies today are decided by external elements. Juveniles tend to follow activities that are prevailing on social media, and this trend not only spreads quickly but also takes over their deliberate time. Moreover, celebrities play a pivotal role. When a singer or athlete promotes a sport or pastime, countless fans immediately imitate them. Last but not least, since online trends shifts rapidly, a wide range of hobbies are likely to be on the verge of fading, indicating that people often follow fashions rather than genuine interests. In summary, despite the existence of individual preferences, the majority of current hobbies are more reflecting trends and fashions. Therefore, social influence is the fundamental factor shaping people’s leisure activities in nowadays."

"Some people believe that patience is not a useful strategy, and immediate actions should always be taken. Personally, I partly agree with this idea because in certain situations prompt action is necessary, but in other cases patience can lead to better results. On the one hand, taking action immediately has several advantages. First, it helps people not to waste opportunities. For instance, in business, if an entrepreneur waits too long, its competitor may develop the same product earlier and dominate the market. In addition, acting quickly can solve urgent problems. A good example is when someone is sick; going to the hospital at once is far better than waiting until the situation becomes worse. These examples show that being proactive is sometimes the best strategy. On the other hand, patience should not be considered useless. In many areas of life, success requires time and consistent effort. For example, learning a foreign language cannot be achieved overnight, and only persistent study and practice can bring long-term improvement. Moreover, in relationships, patience helps people avoid unnecessary conflict. If people act impulsively and say hurtful words without thinking, they may regret it later. Therefore, patience often prevents mistakes and creates better outcomes. Moreover, patience is the remedy of uncertainty and doubt. It facilitates the strengthening of relationships through the process of faith and trust. In conclusion, while there are times when we must act without delay, patience is still an important virtue that brings stability and long-term success. In my opinion, the best approach is to balance both patience and quick action depending on the situation. "

"It is generally the case that numerous individuals have made up their mind to follow a certain career path at a young age. While the majority totally agree this mindset will bring them a wide range of merits, I believe thar other people seek for job satisfaction in different ways. On the one hand, having a defined career path at an early stage can lead to a satisfying working life. Many individuals decide who they want to become when they are still at a young age, and this early determination allows them to stay focused on their goals. As a result, they often experience a great sense of satisfaction when working towards their ambitions and gradually achieving them. For example, many children dream of becoming doctors, but in order to realize this ambition, they need to obtain relevant qualifications and undertake several years of training. Moreover, this is an effective way for those who want to follow in their family’s footsteps, especially when their parents work in traditional or respected professions such as medicine or law. In other words, people who commence preparing for their future careers at an early stage are more likely to become experts in their fields and remain loyalty for their jobs. On the other hand, people can also find joy in their working life in alternative approaches. Firstly, not everyone dreams of sticking to only one job; trying out a variety of professions can be equally rewarding. In fact, commencing a completely new career path might be even a reinvigorating experience that brings new energy and excitement. Secondly, some individuals simply view a job as a way to earn money, and they feel satisfied as long as it can meet their needs or provide increments or financial reward. For example, positive working atmosphere, enthusiastic colleagues, and an inspirational boss can make one’s working life much more contented, regardless of the profession. In conclusion, it is undoubted that committing to a particular profession for a lifetime can lead to job satisfaction, however, this is by no means the sole route to fulfillment."

"It is the case that as technology has developed, people can now travel to remote natural areas. While there are some drawbacks related to this trend, I think that the benefits are much greater. On the one hand, it is obvious that there are a number of significant disadvantages in relation to this development. One of the most evident drawbacks is that travelling to remote natural areas can be risky if the travelers are not sufficiently prepared. For instance, the temperature at the South Pole is usually very low, which adversely affects people's health. Furthermore, travelling to forests can also be dangerous as people have to face the risk of being attacked by wild animals such as carnivores. Another negative aspect that should not be overlooked is that since visiting isolated places often requires a large amount of investment in researching and ensuring the safety of travelers, the costs of travelling tend to be high. To be more specific, it seems like only scientists and wealthy people can afford this activity, so this development is likely to benefit only a small group of individuals. On the other hand, I think that the positively far-reaching impacts outnumber the risks involved. To begin with, it is undoubted that a newer and more interesting type of travelling is the first benefit. In other words, going to other cities or countries has been too common for most people, so it might be more exciting for them to explore new places such as the South Pole or the Amazon rainforest. Not only can This give them valuable experiences, but they can also be unforgettable memories. Another potential benefit is that when visiting remote areas, people, especially scientists, might acquire more knowledge about the natural habitat. For example, when coming to the North Pole, scientists can learn about the life of polar bears which live far away from humans.In short, while there are some potential drawbacks of being able to travel to remote places, my perspective is that the benefits are much more obvious."

"The trade-off between enhancing industrial skills or education in order to develop a country has always been a confusing topic among many people. While some claim that the former should come first, others and I argue that the latter should be a priority. There are several reasons why industrial skills should be improved. It is undoubted that since developing countries often lack workers with specialised ability, the governments of these countries should invest more in training and enhancing working skills to provide sufficient work force. For example, in Vietnam, there are currently very few people who work in the automobile manufacturing industry, so it is necessary to increase the number of workers in this field. In addition, it is obvious that when workers’ practical skills are improved, the productivity of factories would be enhanced. As a result, more goods with higher qualities would be produced, which contributes to the national budget for the whole nation. On the other hand, other people and I are of the opinion that education would be a better option/alternative for a more stable growth in developing countries. The first reason is that since education provides basic knowledge for people, it is likely to assist in the development of other industries. For instance, education can help scientists to create new technologies, which allows workers to produce modern devices such as smart phones. The second obvious reason is that improving the education system would produce good citizens. This means that, being well educated, people would be more likely to have good behaviors such as obeying the law, and this contributes to the development of the entire society. If inadequate attention is paid to education, the growth of countries will only be short-term. In conclusion, while some may think that developing skills for industrialization should be prioritized, I would contend that enhancing education will bring about a stable and long-term solution."

"It is the globally case that more and more married couples do not want to have children at the early post-marriage life. There are some factors related to this tendency, and I think that it brings both positives and negatives effects. To begin with, there are several grounds accounting for an increasing number of individuals tending to have children later in their life. Firstly, this choice allows them to have more time fund to be engaged to personal enjoyable pursuits, not only vigorously but also sedentarily. For instance, it is easier for a young couple to travel when they do not have a baby to nurture. Secondly, people who are at the very early stage of their career usually have lower income compared to when they are older. Therefore, many of them decide to start raising a family after having accumulated sufficient budget to ensure/ guarantee a better life for their prospective children. However, this tendency might cause some negative influences on both family life and society. The first impact is that having children later will cause a significant age gap between two generations. This makes it harder for parents to understand and communicate with their offspring. Additionally, if people give birth when they are too old, the babies being born might be less physically healthy. This might adversely affect the quality of the future workforce if too many people decide to follow this trend. In conclusion, there are some reasons why many newly-married couples postpone having kids, and this tendency can result in some negative impacts on both their personal life and the whole outer community."

"In the contemporary context, leaving school without a diploma has been prevailing in our society. It is supposed that the majority of students usually make their own decision on this, whilst the uncertainly subsequent success will depend largely on personal efforts, favorable conditions, and even fortune. First of all, there are a vast range of reasons why the number of people dropping out has been increasing over years. From their viewpoint, never before has their studying in school been either prioritized or pre-empted in their orientation of career. Thus, they could be able to consider other paths; for example, workers can enter the labor market by the age of fifteen to make ends meet and reduce the burden with their guardians. The next issue could originate from their parents’ decisions. Numerous mothers and fathers impose their thoughts on their offsprings, initiating perceptual gaps among them. As a result, a wrong choice could be made regardless, following that, stopping attending school could be an escape. Secondly, it can be obviously seen that there are a variety of factors leading to success. As mentioned previously, personal efforts play a pivotal role and determine our fate entirely. In spite of not attending class, we can broaden our horizons thanks to social knowledge through working environment, and predecessors' experiments. Furthermore, we can develop ourselves comprehensively and overcome obstacles. Nevertheless, it is necessary for us to find out in advance what will be relevant to our intention as preparation for the upcoming time. As for all of us, that fortune is one of the most indispensable elements contributes to successful journeys. To conclude, there are two sides for everything and dropping out is not the exception. It is absolutely advisable that we should take responsibility for what we decide and try our best so as not to regret."

"Nowadays, there is a wide range of ongoing controversy related to the legal driving age. While numerous citizens argue that raising minimum legal age is an optimal solution to minimize significantly risks regarding traffic, I am convinced that alternative measures may prove to be more comprehensive and effective. On the one hand, there are several compelling reasons for this viewpoint. First of all, it is the fact that adolescent drivers often lack the emotional maturity and problem-solving skills, especially in some unexpected situations. For example, the more frightening and unpredictable the case is, the more likely it is that teenagers fail to stay calm and become struggle, which can lead to unwise decisions when participating in traffic. Besides, they do not have enough deeper insights about traffic regulation; and therefore, they are not fully aware of the seriousness as well as consequences caused by their actions. However, age does not accurately reflect a person’s driving ability or sense of responsibility. For instance, numerous older drivers also exhibit reckless behavior such as speeding or violating traffic legislation, so increasing the age requirement may not be a best way to reduce traffic accidents. Rather than focusing on age only, governing bodies and authorities should invest in driving programs that not only teach youngsters how to operate a vehicle properly, but also raise their awareness of traffic law, dangerous situations, and responsible behavior on the road. In conclusion, raising the driving age is not the greatest measure to make roads safer. It would be more effective to teach adolescent drivers how to operate their vehicles carefully and take responsibility for their actions."

"Nowadays, there are numerous debates which are related to the penalty for junior offenders. A great deal of individuals argue that they should be receive the same punishment and treatment as adult criminals, while I and others firmly believe that this situation tends to bring youngsters more demerits than merits. On the one hand, there are several benefits for this opinion. First of all, treating teens like adults can enhance the equality in social community. This approach ensures that all law-breakers have to be responsible for their morally unacceptable actions, regardless of ages. In addition, because of their reliant on tolerant legislation, young wrongdoers may become more violent and cause serious accidents, even killing innocent lives. Therefore, thanks to heavy penalties and deterrent measures, youngsters are likely to be affair and make an effort to have the excellent reformation, which helps them be released sooner. On the other hand, it is said that disadvantages for this outweigh the obvious advantages. This is because juveniles have not fully developed in term of both cognition and maturity, so it is easy for them to lose their self-control, leading to a variety of unforeseen consequences. Moreover, teenagers represent a foundation of a country’s development; and therefore, punishing them as extremely as adults can destroy not only their chance to correct mistakes but also their potential to become valuable citizens. From my perspective, governing bodies and authorities should not adopt to this measure and show a tolerance for offenders who are under the legal age. They need support and guidance rather than severe punishment, so that they can acknowledge their wrongdoing and improve to be a better version."
